budget_wines.name: Tio Pepe Fino Muy Seco
budget_wines.typical_price: 8 EUR at Spanish supermarket; 10-12 EUR at UK wine shop
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Gonzalez Byass bodega shop, Calle Manuel Maria Gonzalez 12, Jerez de la Frontera; widely stocked at supermarkets across Spain, UK and Germany.
budget_wines.description: The world's best-selling fino sherry. Dry, saline, almond-touched; aged under flor in the historic Gonzalez Byass solera in Jerez. An extraordinary value at under ten euros.
budget_wines.tip: Serve in a chilled narrow copita at 7-9 degrees Celsius alongside salted almonds or jamón ibérico.

budget_wines.name: La Gitana Manzanilla
budget_wines.typical_price: 8 EUR at Spanish supermarket; 9-12 EUR at specialist wine shops
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Hidalgo La Gitana bodega, Banda Playa 42, Sanlucar de Barrameda; widely distributed across Spain, UK, Germany, Benelux, US.
budget_wines.description: Among the most widely distributed Manzanillas internationally. Crisp, bone-dry, ocean-salty with chamomile and green apple; aged under flor in Sanlucar de Barrameda's Atlantic humidity. Strong value.
budget_wines.tip: A classic entry-point Manzanilla for introducing guests to the style; pour very cold and finish the bottle within a few days of opening.

budget_wines.name: Lustau Don Nuno Dry Oloroso
budget_wines.typical_price: 11 EUR at specialist Spanish wine shop; 13-16 EUR in UK
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Lustau Bodega, Plaza del Cubo 4, Jerez de la Frontera; widely distributed across Europe and North America.
budget_wines.description: Full, rich, dry oloroso from Emilio Lustau's solera. Walnut, dried fig, dark chocolate and leather aromas; medium-bodied with a long nutty finish. Outstanding value in the dry fortified category.
budget_wines.tip: Pair with manchego, Iberian charcuterie, or dark chocolate; serves well slightly cool at 14-16 degrees Celsius.

verified.address_quoted: Plaza del Cubo 4, Jerez de la Frontera

budget_wines.name: Barbadillo Solear Manzanilla
budget_wines.typical_price: 9 EUR at Spanish wine shops; 11-14 EUR in northern European markets
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Barbadillo bodega shop, Calle Sevilla 1, Sanlucar de Barrameda; available at specialist wine shops across Spain, UK and Germany.
budget_wines.description: The flagship Manzanilla of Bodegas Barbadillo, Sanlucar de Barrameda's largest producer. Pale gold, saline, with delicate floral notes and a clean dry finish. Consistent year-round expression of the Manzanilla style.
budget_wines.tip: Ask for the Solear en Rama spring edition when available; the unfiltered version shows the style at its most expressive.

budget_wines.name: Williams and Humbert Dry Sack Medium Oloroso
budget_wines.typical_price: 10 EUR at Spanish wine shops; 12-15 EUR in UK and German markets
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Williams and Humbert bodega, Carretera Nacional IV, km 641.75, Jerez de la Frontera; widely distributed across Europe.
budget_wines.description: A classic semi-seco medium oloroso blend of Palomino and Pedro Ximenez, aged in the Williams and Humbert solera system. Rich, round, with dried fruit sweetness balanced by walnut bitterness and a long amber finish.
budget_wines.tip: Serve slightly chilled as an aperitivo or with blue cheese and walnuts.

verified.address_quoted: Carretera Nacional IV, Jerez de la Frontera

budget_wines.name: Osborne Fino Quinta
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Osborne bodega shop, Calle Los Moros 7, El Puerto de Santa Maria; available at Spanish supermarkets and specialist shops.
budget_wines.description: Osborne's entry-level fino from El Puerto de Santa Maria, light and dry with almond, citrus zest and a saline mineral finish. Aged under flor in the Osborne solera, one of the oldest in the Triangle.
budget_wines.tip: A reliable fino at a bargain price; order it in any bar in El Puerto and it will be freshly poured from an open bottle that day.

budget_wines.name: Argueso San Leon Manzanilla Reserva
budget_wines.typical_price: 10 EUR at Sanlucar specialist shops; 13-16 EUR in export markets
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Bodegas Argueso, Calle Mar 8, Sanlucar de Barrameda; available via specialist sherry importers in UK, Germany and the United States.
budget_wines.description: A slightly aged manzanilla pasada (between standard manzanilla and amontillado in character) from the Argueso solera in Sanlucar. More oxidative and nutty than a young manzanilla while retaining flor freshness. Distinctive toasted bread, dried chamomile and sea salt profile.
budget_wines.tip: San Leon Reserva is the step up from La Gitana for those who want more depth without losing Sanlucar salinity.

budget_wines.name: Sanchez Romate Cardenal Cisneros Pedro Ximenez
budget_wines.typical_price: 10 EUR at Jerez wine shops for the 50cl bottle
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Sanchez Romate bodega, Calle Lealas 26, Jerez de la Frontera; specialist wine retailers across Spain and northern Europe.
budget_wines.description: Rich, deeply concentrated Pedro Ximenez from one of Jerez's oldest independent bodegas. Jet-black colour, aromas of dried figs, raisins, coffee and dark chocolate; 400-plus grams per litre of natural sweetness. One of the best-value PX expressions in the Sherry Triangle.
budget_wines.tip: Serve a small pour over good vanilla ice cream for the simplest luxury dessert in Andalucia.

verified.address_quoted: Calle Lealas 26, Jerez de la Frontera

budget_wines.name: Pedro Romero Aurora Manzanilla
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Bodegas Pedro Romero, Calle Trasbolsa 84, Sanlucar de Barrameda; available through specialist importers in the UK and Germany.
budget_wines.description: A light, delicate Manzanilla from the venerable Pedro Romero bodega in Sanlucar, producers of some of the most traditional Atlantic-influenced sherries in the appellation. Pale straw colour, fresh brine and white flower aromas, clean and dry on the palate.
budget_wines.tip: One of the best values at local Sanlucar bars; ask for it by name for a more traditional Manzanilla than the mass-market brands.

budget_wines.name: Williams and Humbert Leyenda Amontillado
budget_wines.typical_price: 11 EUR at Spanish wine shops; 13-17 EUR in export markets
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Williams and Humbert bodega shop, Jerez de la Frontera; specialist sherry importers across Europe.
budget_wines.description: A medium-aged amontillado with amber colour, hazelnuts, dried apricot and a characteristically long saline finish. The Leyenda range from Williams and Humbert represents some of the most consistent and fairly priced amontillado in the Jerez market.
budget_wines.tip: Amontillado is the most food-versatile sherry style; serve slightly chilled with charcuterie, strong cheeses or roast chicken.

verified.address_quoted: Carretera Nacional IV, Jerez de la Frontera
